title: T-008 — scheduler_config table (PLE-005)
prompt:
# T-008 — scheduler_config table (PLE-005)

PRD: 07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md §17.1 PLE-005, §17.3 backlog item 1
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Introduce `scheduler_config` table holding per-tenant default run parameters and alert thresholds (PLE-005 Cards 1-2 persistence target).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0077_scheduler_config.sql`: CREATE TABLE scheduler_config per PLE-005 DDL stub (tenant_id PK, default_horizon_days, default_include_forecast, solver_timeout_seconds, alerts JSONB, updated_by, updated_at) + RLS.
2. CHECK default_horizon_days IN (7, 14) and solver_timeout_seconds BETWEEN 10 AND 300.
3. RLS policy on tenant_id.
4. Drizzle schema entry.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0077_scheduler_config.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/scheduler_config.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/scheduler-config.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0077 applied, when \d scheduler_config runs, then all PLE-005 columns + horizon CHECK + timeout CHECK + RLS exist
2. Given an INSERT with default_horizon_days=21, when executed, then it fails CHECK
3. Given two tenants, when each SELECTs, then each only sees its own config row

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/scheduler-config.test.ts asserting CHECK + RLS + tenant_id PK
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test scheduler-config'

## Risk red lines
- Do not skip CHECK constraints (UI relies on these for validation rendering)
- Do not skip RLS
- Do not forget to make tenant_id the PRIMARY KEY (one row per tenant)

pipeline_inputs.description: Create scheduler_config table per PLE-005 with horizon and timeout CHECK constraints + RLS.
